subject: Hematology
question: A 60-year-old woman 3 days post-hip replacement develops unilateral leg swelling and pain. Wells score is 4. What is the best next step?

options.text: Start empiric anticoagulation + order Doppler ultrasound
options.explanation: Wells score ≥2 indicates likely DVT. Start empiric anticoagulation (DOAC or LMWH) while awaiting diagnostic confirmation. Doppler ultrasound confirms. Early treatment prevents PE.

options.text: Wait for Doppler ultrasound before treatment
options.explanation: NEVER delay anticoagulation awaiting ultrasound if high clinical suspicion (Wells ≥2). Start treatment immediately. Ultrasound may take hours. Risk of fatal PE during delay.
D-dimer is for LOW probability (Wells ≤1). Wells ≥2 = high probability, skip D-dimer, go straight to imaging and treatment. D-dimer will be elevated post-surgery regardless.
Aspirin is NOT adequate for DVT treatment. Requires therapeutic anticoagulation (DOAC, warfarin, or LMWH). Aspirin is for arterial thrombosis, not venous.
Compression stockings are for PREVENTION of post-thrombotic syndrome, not acute DVT treatment. May dislodge thrombus and cause PE if used without anticoagulation.

educationalObjective: Start empiric anticoagulation immediately if high probability DVT (Wells ≥2). Do not delay treatment for diagnostic confirmation.
keyTakeaway: DVT: Wells ≥2 = start anticoagulation NOW + order Doppler. DOAC first-line. LMWH/warfarin if DOAC contraindicated. Treat 3-6 months.
